title: Hydriastele manusii
taxonomy: Hydriastele manusii is classified within the family Arecaceae, commonly known as palm trees. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as its palmate leaves and solitary inflorescence. The genus Hydriastele is a part of the subfamily Calamoideae, which includes various species of climbing palms. As a member of the Arecaceae family, Hydriastele manusii shares similarities with other palm species in terms of its growth habit and leaf morphology. The exact classification of Hydriastele manusii may vary depending on the taxonomic source or recent revisions in the plant kingdom.

intro: Hydriastele manusii, also known as the 'Fiji fan palm', is a species of palm tree native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Hydriastele manusii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
